title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast explores social engineering attacks against AI systems, including prompt injection techniques, self-replicating AI worms from University of Toronto, and the nonhuman identity (NHI) security problem. The discussion examines whether AI agents are more vulnerable to manipulation than humans and addresses inadequate auditing of nonhuman credentials.
writeup: An IBM Security Intelligence podcast episode examines whether AI systems can be manipulated through social engineering and how that risk compares to manipulating humans. The discussion covers prompt injection attacks targeting Meta and Instagram, a self-replicating AI worm developed at the University of Toronto, and identity security risks tied to unaudited nonhuman credentials. It connects these cases to broader concerns about malware distribution, multi-turn reasoning, and the security implications of autonomous AI agents.
